title: Twist gesture for compass calibration
design_brief: A figure-8 / twist gesture for calibrating an in-app compass — visualize finger path with breadcrumb dots.
mobile_reasoning: Pan tracks finger; deposit a Skia dot every N pixels. Track total angular sweep; on full rotation, success haptic.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const TWIST = {dotSpacing: 18};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Dots fade as they age.
gesture_strategy: Pan.
interaction_details: Two full rotations → success haptic + callback
accessibility_considerations: Gesture-only behavior is mirrored by accessibilityActions and a visible label.
performance_notes: Gesture values stay on the UI thread through shared values; React state is not updated per frame.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Continuous gesture sampling. 2) Worklet detection.

title: Sheet that promotes to full screen on swipe up
design_brief: Bottom sheet that, when dragged to top snap, transitions to a full-screen presentation (header, status bar takeover).
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Sheet that doesn't actually feel full-screen
mobile_reasoning: At top snap, interpolate header opacity + status bar style transition. StatusBar.setBarStyle.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: Status bar handling.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Interpolations on snap progress.
gesture_strategy: Header pan controls the sheet; close remains available as an explicit button for non-gesture users.
interaction_details: Crossing full snap: header fades in + status bar light
accessibility_considerations: Sheet uses accessibilityViewIsModal and includes an explicit close action.
performance_notes: Translation and backdrop opacity are derived on the UI thread with one shared value.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Status bar promotion. 2) Header transition.

title: FlashList horizontal carousel with item snapping
design_brief: Horizontal carousel with snap to item. Reference: Apple App Store featured carousel.
mobile_reasoning: snapToInterval + decelerationRate='fast'.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Native scroll.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Half-peek of next card visible at edges
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: FlashList uses stable keys, memoized rows, and estimated sizes to keep scrolling predictable.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Native snap. 2) Page-edge peek.

title: Toggle group: radio-style selection
design_brief: A horizontal group of buttons where only one can be selected at a time. Reference: settings 'Auto / Light / Dark'.
mobile_reasoning: Segmented control from rn-072 OR distinct option buttons.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Selection animates.
gesture_strategy: Tap targets are at least 44px and form submission is explicit; no hidden gesture is required.
accessibility_considerations: Each input has a label, error hint, visible validation copy, and disabled submit state.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Per-platform haptic. 2) Solid radio pattern.

title: Universal links opening specific routes
design_brief: https://myapp.com/post/123 opens app to /post/123 if installed.
mobile_reasoning: iOS: Associated Domains entitlement + apple-app-site-association on server. Android: assetlinks.json + intent filter with autoVerify.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: This task IS the difference.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
solution: // app.json\n// {\"expo\": {\"ios\": {\"associatedDomains\": [\"applinks:myapp.com\"]}, \"android\": {\"intentFilters\": [{\"action\": \"VIEW\", \"autoVerify\": true, \"data\": [{\"scheme\": \"https\", \"host\": \"myapp.com\", \"pathPattern\": \"/post/.*\"}], \"category\": [\"BROWSABLE\", \"DEFAULT\"]}]}}}\n// Server: https://myapp.com/.well-known/apple-app-site-association and https://myapp.com/.well-known/assetlinks.json
interaction_details: Falls back to Safari if not installed
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Universal Links / App Links — OS routing.

what_makes_this_native: Uses native platform primitives rather than web-only assumptions, so touch, keyboard, safe areas, and accessibility stay coherent.

title: Stream graph: stacked area variant
design_brief: Stacked area chart for time-series breakdown.
mobile_reasoning: Multiple area paths in Skia.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: On-mount draw.
gesture_strategy: Tap or focus a data mark to expose the exact value; the semantic legend mirrors the same interaction for screen readers.
accessibility_considerations: Chart has an image summary plus focusable legend rows, so the data is understandable without color or touch precision.
performance_notes: Geometry is memoized and Skia renders marks on the native canvas, avoiding React work during interaction.
what_makes_this_native: Uses native platform primitives rather than web-only assumptions, so touch, keyboard, safe areas, and accessibility stay coherent.

title: Mineral gradient: deep blues to subtle purples
design_brief: Linear gradient background with mineral/gem-like palette.
mobile_reasoning: LinearGradient with multi-stop colors.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: Uses native platform primitives rather than web-only assumptions, so touch, keyboard, safe areas, and accessibility stay coherent.

mobile_reasoning: useFrameCallback samples frame timing.
